name: EA Solution Architecture — PATH/HAIL Convergence
description: Support Architecture Review Board (ARB) assessment of HC/PHAC enterprise AI platform strategy convergence.

scope.primary_question: Should PATH (HC-led governance control plane) and HAIL (PHAC-led operational runtime) merge into a single governed platform or remain co-equal builds with defined integration points?
target_audience: EA team members and solution architects

deliverables.name: PATH/HAIL Convergence Framing Document
deliverables.description: Three explicit convergence options with tradeoff analysis and EA-recommended position for ARB decision-making.
deliverables.success_gate: ARB can articulate tradeoffs and move to decision without re-interpretation

deliverables.name: WAF Assessment — Operational Excellence & Reliability
deliverables.description: Formalize WAF assessment for operational excellence and reliability pillars. Document gaps addressable within each convergence scenario.
deliverables.success_gate: Security and finance stakeholders confirm risk characterization

deliverables.name: Risk Register — AI Platform Convergence Risks
deliverables.description: Formalize risk register with convergence scenario impact analysis and escalation triggers.
deliverables.success_gate: Risk register tells coherent story; DG understands institutional impact

deliverables.name: ADR-002 — Protected B Hosting Architecture
deliverables.description: Resolve architectural decision on PATH Protected B hosting model with decision context, options, and rationale.
deliverables.success_gate: Security and cloud ops leads sign off on decision rationale

deliverables.name: Shadow AI Risk Narrative
deliverables.description: Connect PATH/HAIL convergence question to business risk of workloads routing to ungoverned platforms.
deliverables.success_gate: PMRA stakeholders recognize their use case; briefing-ready for DM/ADM

convergence_scenarios.name: Single Governed Platform (Converge)
convergence_scenarios.description: HAIL becomes runtime layer; PATH becomes governance control plane; single landing zone, unified FinOps, single ATO path.
key_tradeoffs: Longer path to production (requires PATH maturity)

convergence_scenarios.name: Co-Equal Platforms with Defined Integration Points
convergence_scenarios.description: PATH and HAIL remain separate; governance convergence at AI Responsible Committee level; HAIL handles runtime, PATH handles governance.

convergence_scenarios.name: PHAC-Led Single Platform (Defer PATH)
convergence_scenarios.description: Use HAIL as sole enterprise platform; incorporate PATH governance vision into HAIL's operational model.
key_tradeoffs: Requires HAIL to scale beyond current maturity
Reduces HC voice in governance design

validation_topics: Use case priority and timeline sensitivity
Alignment with DTB strategy and cluster model

quality_standards.audience_assumption: ARB includes finance, compliance, and non-architect decision-makers
quality_standards.evidence_requirement: All claims grounded in documented source material
quality_standards.decision_readiness: ARB moves to decision without requiring clarification

success_metrics: ARB has three explicit convergence options with clear tradeoff analysis
EA recommended position is stated and defensible
Stakeholders (security, cloud ops, PMRA, EA leadership) reviewed and provided sign-off
Risk register is current with three P1 items that convergence decision mitigates
ADR-002 completed and linked from convergence framing document
